channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Building a Real-Time HTTP Anomaly Detection Engine for Nextcloud with Python, Nginx, and iptables
item.description: For this project, I deployed Nextcloud behind Nginx and built a Python daemon that performs real-time anomaly detection on incoming HTTP traffic. The key requirement was to avoid static...

item.title: github failed at the only thing they should do: git
item.description: Yes, I know GitHub does a lot more than Git these days. Actions, packages, security tooling, issue tracking, enterprise management, AI features, code search, and half the software industry glued...

item.title: Running Azure Logic Apps Standard on Azure Container Apps
item.description: Should you use Logic Apps Standard on ACA instead of n8n? n8n is popular for workflow automation — Docker-native, visual editor, hundreds of integrations. But if you're already in Azure, it means...

item.title: Database Migrations — Zero-Downtime SQL, Alembic & Schema Evolution (2026)
item.description: Skip to main content BACKEND ARCHITECTURE MASTERY Day 12: The SQL of Evolution — Alembic & Zero-Downtime Migrations ⏱️ 18 min read Series: Logic & Legacy Day 12 / 30 Level: Senior Architecture ⏳...

item.title: AI Governance in Practice: FastAPI on EKS with Model Cards, Audit Logging, and Helm
item.description: AI governance is increasingly a business requirement, not an afterthought. Whether it's the EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, or an internal risk committee, the question is the same: can you prove your...

item.title: When AI Says "Done", What Is Done?
item.description: Yesterday your AI agent finished the task. The logs were clean. No errors. No warnings. Task count: complete. Then you opened the code. Three items marked done had never been implemented. One...

item.title: Retrieval Augmented Localization Cuts LLM Terminology Errors 17-45%
item.description: Production localization translates isolated paragraphs and strings. A CI/CD pipeline diffs against the previous version and retranslates what changed — a UI string, a tooltip, a modified...

item.title: Retrospective: How We Cut Feature Flag Rollout Time by 70% with LaunchDarkly 5.0 and Argo Rollouts 1.7
item.description: In Q3 2024, our 12-person platform team reduced mean feature flag rollout time from 14.2 minutes to 4.26 minutes—a 70% reduction—by integrating LaunchDarkly 5.0’s new progressive rollout API with...

item.title: Docker Ate Your Disk: Reclaiming 20-50GB on macOS
item.description: Docker Desktop was using 67GB on my Mac. I didn't notice until my build failed with "no space left on device" and I started digging. 67GB. For containers I hadn't run in months, images I'd pulled...

item.title: I built a local-first Chrome extension for translating selected text and saving vocabulary
item.description: I recently built Vocabinet, a Chrome extension for people who read foreign-language content online and want a faster way to translate words or phrases without leaving the page. The idea came from...

item.title: The keyboard used to score Dune 2 is getting more affordable
item.description: Expressive E is making its Osmose MPE keyboard (comparatively) more affordable by ditching the synthesizer and making it a simple MIDI controller. The new Osmose CE comes in two sizes, 49 and 61...

item.title: Larry’s risky business
item.description: If you want to know whether the AI bubble is bursting, there's only one publicly traded company that will tell you: Oracle. That's right, the database company. Oracle has burned its boats and...

item.title: Firestorm Labs raises $82M to take drone factories into the field
item.description: A defense startup just raised $82 million to put drone factories inside shipping containers and bring manufacturing to the front lines.

item.title: Meet Shapes, the app bringing humans and AI into the same group chats
item.description: Think Discord chats, but with AI characters in addition to humans.

item.title: Taylor Swift deepfakes are pushing scams on TikTok
item.description: Scammers are using AI-generated videos of celebrities including Taylor Swift and Rihanna to promote shady services on TikTok, according to authentication company Copyleaks. The ads typically show...

item.title: The Download: storing nuclear waste and orchestrating agents
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. It’s time to make a plan for nuclear waste Today, nuclear...

item.title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
item.description: In February 2026, researchers uncovered a shift that completely changed the game: threat actors are now using custom AI setups to automate attacks directly into the kill chain. We aren't just...

item.title: Electric air taxis are finally taking flight — just not with passengers
item.description: It was the perfect day for an electric air taxi demonstration - albeit one without passengers. On Monday, an electric aircraft from Joby Aviation took off from JFK Airport for a flight...

item.title: Saros reminded me how great the DualSense can be
item.description: The DualSense controller was one of Sony's main selling points for the PS5. Beyond things like a striking new design and adaptive triggers, the controller's haptic feedback offered genuinely cool...

item.title: Your PS5 can now transform into a Linux PC
item.description: A developer has created a method to get Linux running on some versions of Sony's PlayStation 5 console. Andy Nguyen previously showed off a ported version of Ubuntu running PC games on a PS5 last...

item.title: What to Look for in an Exposure Management Platform (And What Most of Them Get Wrong)
item.description: Every security team has a version of the same story. The quarter ends with hundreds of vulnerabilities closed. The dashboards are bursting with green. Then someone in a leadership meeting asks:...

item.title: Why a recent supply-chain attack singled out security firms Checkmarx and Bitwarden
item.description: Security firms find themselves especially exposed.

item.title: Meta isn’t doing enough to keep kids off Facebook and Instagram, rules EU
item.description: Meta is breaching Europe's Digital Services Act (DSA) rules by failing to prevent children under 13 from using Facebook and Instagram, according to a preliminary decision issued by the European...

item.title: China freezes new robotaxi licenses after Baidu chaos
item.description: China has suspended new licenses for autonomous vehicles, Bloomberg reports, citing unnamed people familiar with the matter. The move comes after dozens of robotaxis operated by Chinese tech giant...

item.title: Claude Mythos Has Found 271 Zero-Days in Firefox
item.description: That’s a lot. No, it’s an extraordinary number: Since February, the Firefox team has been working around the clock using frontier AI models to find and fix latent security vulnerabilities in the...

item.title: GitHub rushed to fix a critical vulnerability in less than six hours
item.description: GitHub employees fixed a critical remote code execution vulnerability in less than six hours last month. Wiz Research used AI models to uncover a vulnerability in GitHub's internal git...

item.title: I've Covered Robots for Years. This One Is Different
item.description: From sorting chicken nuggets to screwing in lightbulbs, Eka’s robotic claw feels like we're approaching a ChatGPT moment for the physical world.

item.title: Colby Adcock’s Scout AI raises $100M to train its models for war. We visited its bootcamp
item.description: We visited Scout AI's training ground where it's working on AI agents that can help individual soldiers control fleets of autonomous vehicles.

item.title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
item.description: cPanel has released security updates to address a security issue impacting various authentication paths that could allow an attacker to obtain access to the control panel software. The problem...

item.title: How AI Could Help Combat Antibiotic Resistance
item.description: At WIRED Health, British surgeon Ara Darzi said AI is set to transform the diagnosis and treatment of drug-resistant infections. But a lack of incentives means innovation may not reach patients.
